biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:SLIGHT_IMPROVEMENT — violations in ~8 of 18 substantive tool-using units (~44%): 03e5a89c-2 (ls -la Bash at session entry fails success=false); 03e5a89c-3 (ls -la Bash x2 for bin/docs/modelfiles/presets); 41a73142-0 (ls -la compound Bash fails success=false at session entry); 41a73142-1 (ls -la + find compound Bash); e25e3b92-5 (sed -n '15,35p' instead of Read with offset; cat USAGE.md | head -40 instead of Read); e25e3b92-6 (cat src/utils/env/env.utils.ts | head -80 instead of Read; sed -n '60,140p' next.config.mjs instead of Read; sed -n '1,20p' src/core/app-init.utils.ts fails success=false; sed -n '36,80p' channels.py instead of Read); e25e3b92-12 (sed -n '64,180p' doc-writing-guidelines.md instead of Read with offset); 03e5a89c-27 (sed -n '1,14p' personas files instead of Read). Rate ~44% — first measurable downward signal across 11 consecutive flagged cycles (prior range 47–71%); marginal and not statistically reliable given sample size but represents the first positive data point in recorded history for this pattern.

compound_command_overreach:RECURRING:STABLE — 03e5a89c-3: three distinct compound Bash chains (wrappers+launchctl+lm+wc) all fail success=false before partial decomposition adopted; 03e5a89c-4: bash -n compound fails success=false, Monitor tool misused as polling loop (also fails), bash -n compound fails second time; 41a73142-0: session-entry ls-la compound fails success=false. Decompose-only-after-failure sequence structurally identical to all prior cycles; pre-decomposition behavior still absent across all observed instances.

hook_bypass_for_convenience:RECURRING:STABLE — 41a73142-1: touch ~/.claude/.no-dup-symbol-guard executed after three consecutive Edit failures on src/render.ts; guard restored via trash only after all edits complete. Fourth confirmed instance (prior: 2026-06-12, 2026-06-13 x2, 2026-06-15). Trigger condition (consecutive Edit failures), mechanism (touch .no-*-guard), and late-restoration behavior structurally identical across all four instances. Correct response (re-read file, identify conflict, resolve without disabling guard) was never attempted in any confirmed instance.

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — e25e3b92-5: approximately 28 Edit calls on create-upload.md with ~8 success=false failures; one mid-thrash Read executed (marginal improvement vs prior cycles where re-read never happened during thrash); python3 inline splice still adopted as exit mechanism rather than resolving the edit conflict directly. Single-unit occurrence — below the two-unit 2026-06-13 peak; matches 2026-06-15 single-unit cycle. No improvement in detection-and-resolution behavior.

declared_ready_without_verification:RECURRING:STABLE — 03e5a89c-5: input topic 'tested verification checklist' (message_length 44, terse); only 3 tool calls (TaskUpdate + 2 Edits, zero Bash execution, zero output observation); outcome Corrected. Structurally identical to all prior confirmed instances. [declared-ready] mistake-pattern tag from rules/testing.md matches.

rg_replace_flag_bundle:RECURRING:STABLE — e25e3b92-6: confirmed recurrence (agent self-reported 4th but full calibration history records this as 5th+): rg -rn SKIP_ENV_VARIABLES_CHECK treated as --replace flag bundle producing 'process.env.n'; self-caught from improbable identifier shape before any write; ATONE event mist-20260612-110132-40 refiled; prop-20260612-110331-da hook enforcement proposal exists but remains unimplemented across five sessions. Self-catch rate 100% across all confirmed instances; prevention rate zero. Spec-level rule, session briefing restatement, and per-session ATONE all demonstrably insufficient; hook enforcement is the only unexhausted mechanism.

mandatory_doc_guidelines_not_loaded:RECURRING — Graduated from NEW (flagged 2026-06-13) to RECURRING this cycle. Violation in e25e3b92-5 (authored job-creation-upload.md ~310 lines + edits to data-history.md without loading either MANDATORY doc-writing-guidelines file); remediation in e25e3b92-12 (guidelines loaded, ATONE S3 filed, full voice pass executed across both docs). Self-catch-and-remediate within the same session is a marginal improvement over the prior-cycle detection pattern, but the initial violation still occurred; pattern not resolved.

monitor_tool_misuse_for_syntax_check:RECURRING — 03e5a89c-4: Monitor invoked as 'until bash -n bin/imagine && bash -n bin/q; do sleep 5; done; echo syntax OK' — Monitor is for event-streaming from long-running background processes, not polling trivial synchronous shell commands. Fails success=false. Previously flagged as NEW in 2026-06-13 calibration cycle for identical invocation pattern; now confirmed RECURRING.
cross_references: dream-insight-git-push-approval: 41a73142-1 executed a git commit — message was explicit, user reaction Accepted; no terse-signal ambiguity violation detected. The insight's boundary condition (terse 'yes'/'do it' preceding a push proposal) was not triggered this cycle.

dream-insight-data-hallucination-credential-leakage: no data fabrication events or credential write events observed across all 20 units this cycle. e25e3b92-6 wrote .env.example files but these were documented placeholder templates, not secret values; secret-scan was explicitly run and passed.
recommendations: rg_replace_flag_bundle: implement hook prop-20260612-110331-da immediately — six consecutive cycles of self-catch with zero prevention; spec-level rule has demonstrably failed across sessions and model versions; mechanical PreToolUse guard is the only unexhausted mechanism. Deprioritize any further spec-level guidance for this pattern.

hook_bypass_for_convenience: add PreToolUse hook blocking touch ~/.claude/.no-*-guard patterns — four confirmed instances with identical trigger (consecutive Edit failures) and mechanism (guard file creation); correct response (re-read + resolve conflict) was never attempted in any instance; spec-level prohibition demonstrably insufficient.

mandatory_doc_guidelines_not_loaded: promote from atone to a PreToolUse hook gate for Write/Edit on frontend/docs/ paths — graduated from NEW to RECURRING in a single cycle; self-remediation is faster but initial violation still occurs; a session-scoped flag set on Read of the guidelines files, checked on docs/ writes, provides the mechanical gate.

shell_tool_preference_violations: first marginal downward signal observed (~44% vs 47–71% prior); do not declare resolved — one cycle is noise; deprioritize new spec-level guidance (existing prohibition is extensive and demonstrably insufficient); if a mechanical lint hook on sed|cat.*head|ls.*Bash patterns exists verify it fires; if not, this is the next hook candidate after the three above.

compound_command_overreach and monitor_tool_misuse: no improvement after 11+ cycles; deprioritize — existing failure-then-decompose fallback is functional if slow; both patterns are strategy-quality issues with tolerable outcomes (Accepted) rather than calibration failures with user corrections.
